assessment.summary: The current scanner and agent guard should evolve into an incident-centered control plane with a separate response broker and privileged Windows service.

nonNegotiables: LLM output cannot independently authorize containment
Existing scans and Agent Guard remain available during migration
assumptions: No measured latency, resource or false-positive budget was supplied.

opportunities.title: Incident-centered protection control plane
opportunities.summary: Correlate sensor evidence into durable incidents and isolate privileged response authority behind a broker/service boundary.
opportunities.diagnosis: The current supervisor assesses independent events and ends in audit plus notification; it has no durable incident lifecycle, correlation graph or privileged reversible action boundary.

evidence.claim: Events are independently assessed, audited and notified.
The capability surface has scans and controls but no incident lifecycle or response proposal API.
The live stopped-to-running flow reports PID and CLI status without protection coverage, warm-up state or incident workflow.

desiredInvariants: Every detection becomes a durable incident with evidence and state history.
Emergency risk requires deterministic corroboration and cannot be created by LLM output alone.
Every system-changing response is typed, scoped, audited and executed through a least-privilege broker/service boundary.
Destructive actions require explicit user confirmation and Security PIN.
The UI exposes a truthful protection state, coverage, degraded sensors and pending user decisions.

options.title: Harden the current monolith
options.summary: Add incident state and limited actions to the existing Python runtime.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Adds incident state but keeps authority concentrated.

tradeoffs.assessment: Incident handling improves, but the scanner process gains privileged authority.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Threat-model every action and run adversarial replay.

tradeoffs.assessment: No process hop is added; correlation cost is unknown.
One process owns more failure-sensitive behavior.

residualRisks: Ambient authority remains in the long-lived scanner process.

acceptanceCriteria: Incidents persist and actions roll back

options.title: Local response broker plus privileged Windows service
options.summary: Keep UI, collectors and LLM unprivileged; validate typed proposals before a narrow service executes reversible actions.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Incident correlation and an owned action boundary replace the terminal notification path.

tradeoffs.assessment: Removes ambient administrator authority from UI/model paths.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Fuzz IPC and prove unknown/replayed proposals are rejected.

tradeoffs.assessment: Adds serialization and IPC on response actions, not passive collection.
Adds one service and bounded queues.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure combined idle and burst RSS.

tradeoffs.assessment: Separates scanner/UI failures from privileged action execution and rollback.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Crash each component during active containment.

tradeoffs.assessment: Requires service install, health telemetry and recovery tooling.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Test clean install, upgrade, disable and uninstall.

tradeoffs.assessment: Requires new contracts and shadow-mode rollout.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Preserve old capabilities and compare shadow decisions.

residualRisks: The broker and service become critical trusted components.
User-mode collectors still miss some short-lived activity.

acceptanceCriteria: LLM/UI cannot directly perform privileged actions
Every action expires or rolls back
implementationReadiness.rollback: Disable enforcement, restore active controls, remove service.

options.title: Driver-assisted endpoint stack
options.summary: Add minifilter, ETW and WFP visibility/enforcement around the same incident core.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Closes snapshot visibility gaps but still needs user-mode policy and recovery.

tradeoffs.validationPlan: Replay short-lived network and ransomware activity.
Benchmark file, process and network workloads.

tradeoffs.assessment: Adds kernel buffers and service queues.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure nonpaged pool and service RSS.

tradeoffs.assessment: Driver faults can affect the OS.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Driver Verifier and Windows-version stress matrix.

tradeoffs.assessment: Requires signing, compatibility and crash-response programs.

tradeoffs.validationPlan: Prototype one collector after Option 2 stabilizes.

residualRisks: Kernel attack surface and workstation stability risk.

acceptanceCriteria: Measured visibility benefit justifies driver risk
migrationNotes: Do not start before user-mode baselines exist

opportunities.recommendation: Use the broker/service architecture, with a read-only incident core as the first milestone and driver components only when measurements justify them.
